mission.title: n8n: Calling a sub-workflow with a wait by webhook node, don't return the data of the last node, but the node before the wait.
mission.description: Calling a sub-workflow with a wait by webhook node, don't return the data of the last node, but the node before the wait.. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current n8n deployment
steps.description:
Verify your n8n version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
helm list -n n8n 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working n8n installation.

steps.title: Review n8n configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant n8n configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get configmap -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=n8n
```
## Describe the problem/error/question
When calling a sub-workflow containing a Wait (Webhook) node, the return data is incorrect:
- When called as Agent Tool: Returns data from node before Wait
- When called as Workflow: Returns initial input

steps.title: Apply the fix for Calling a sub-workflow with a wait by webhook node, don't…
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get events -n n8n --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Calling a sub-workflow with a wait by webhook node, don't…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Summary

Executing sub workflows with `Execute Sub Workflow`s in `options.waitForSubWorkflow` mode containing `Wait` nodes or "Human in the Loop" `sendAndWait` nodes would make them return wrong data to the parent workflow once execution resumed after the sub workflow resumed from `waiting` state.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with n8n installed or the issue environment reproducible.
